input.prompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

data.content:

completely. What am I doing wrong?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

data.content:
Two separate issues:

## 1. Use `DynamicResource`, not `StaticResource`

`StaticResource` resolves **once** at load time and never updates. Switch to `DynamicResource` so the binding re-evaluates when you swap dictionaries:

```xml
<!-- ❌ Won't update at runtime -->
<Label TextColor="{StaticResource PrimaryText}" />

<!-- ✅ Updates when the merged dictionary changes -->
<Label TextColor="{DynamicResource PrimaryText}" />
```

Then swap themes by replacing the merged dictionary at runtime:

```csharp
var mergedDicts = Application.Current.Resources.MergedDictionaries;
mergedDicts.Clear();
mergedDicts.Add(useDark ? new DarkTheme() : new LightTheme());
```

## 2. Prevent Activity recreation on UI mode change

Android destroys and recreates the Activity when `uiMode` changes. In `MainActivity.cs`, add `UiMode` to `ConfigurationChanges`:

```csharp
[Activity(Theme = "@style/Maui.SplashTheme", MainLauncher = true,
    ConfigurationChanges = ConfigChanges.ScreenSize
        | ConfigChanges.Orientation
        | ConfigChanges.UiMode        // ← add this
        | ConfigChanges.ScreenLayout
        | ConfigChanges.SmallestScreenSize
        | ConfigChanges.Density)]
public class MainActivity : MauiAppCompatActivity { }
```

This tells Android your app handles the change itself, preventing the restart. You can then detect the system toggle and swap dictionaries accordingly.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about two issues with dark mode in their .NET MAUI Android app:

1. **StaticResource doesn't update at runtime** — They're using `StaticResource` to bind colors, but when they switch themes nothing changes.
2. **App restarts when Android dark mode is toggled** — When the user changes the system dark mode setting, the app restarts.

These are common .NET MAUI issues I can answer directly without needing to explore any codebase.

**Issue 1:** `StaticResource` is resolved once at load time and never updates. They need to use `DynamicResource` instead, which re-evaluates when the resource dictionary changes.


**Issue 2:** Android recreates the Activity by default when `uiMode` changes, so they need to add `ConfigChanges.UiMode` to the `Activity` attribute in `MainActivity.cs` and handle the configuration change manually to prevent the restart.
